caption:
Promise.all() fails if ONE promise rejects.

Promise.any() returns the FIRST one that succeeds.

Know the difference. It could save your production app.

📌 SAVE for when you need it.

Drop a 🔥 if you learned something.

#javascript #typescript #webdev #coding #programming #promises #async #frontend #backend #nodejs #codingtips

option_a.label: Promise.all()

option_b.label: Promise.any()

slides.title: It depends on what you need.

cards.label: Promise.all()
cards.title: When you need ALL results
cards.text: Use when every response matters. Loading a dashboard with 5 API calls — you need all 5. But if one API is down, your entire page breaks.

cards.label: Promise.any()
cards.title: When you need the FASTEST success
cards.text: Use for redundancy. Hit 3 CDN mirrors — take whichever responds first. If mirror1 is down, mirror2 saves you. Zero downtime.

slides.takeaway: Promise.all = need everything. Promise.any = need just one.

slides.title: The full Promise family
slides.explanation: 4 methods. 4 different use cases. Most devs only know .all() and .race().

slides.title: Promise methods decoded

items.text: Promise.all → need ALL, fails on ANY rejection
Promise.any → first SUCCESS wins, ignores failures
Promise.allSettled → waits for ALL, never rejects
Promise.race → first to SETTLE wins (success or fail)

slides.prompt: Which one did you not know about? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: AbortController — cancel any fetch in one line
